reviews.review_text:
Mafia: Definitive Edition was such a pleasant surprise. The game looks absolutely beautiful, and as someone experiencing the Mafia franchise for the first time, I really enjoyed the story and characters. It felt like a great introduction to the series.

Got it on sale for like 5 bucks too, which honestly made it even better value. Definitely worth playing if you enjoy story driven games with that classic mafia movie vibe.

I played this game like 20 years ago and it was one of my most memorable games. Great story, nice settings (1930s) in Chicago. Lots of "antique" car to drive. If you love Puzo's Godfather series, there is no better games that portray that particular era than this one. And that haunting intro song. I watched that intro for hundreds of times in the past. Easily one of the best intro. And, when they released a remaster, it was a no brainer for me. The game played as I remembered it. The character textures have been upgraded to almost live-like. Very realistic. I teared up just being able to play this in a modern PC. You owe it to yourself to try this classic.

This game is so peak. 

I am kind of sad that I finished this game in one breath, just 8 hours to finish the plot, best night in my life.

The game mades u to get into their life, to live as a mafia member, I was so interested all the time during missions, and that fukass race costed me so much mental health, I even wanted to drop the game.

Plot 10/10
Characters 10/10
Cars 10/10 
Graphics 10/10
Details 10/10

Overall: PEAK 

Sadly there wont be any Mafia game like this. F Tommy.

This game is a masterpiece of a crime story telling. The story and the characters are incredibly well written, with a very stunningly looking city and environments. The soundtrack is amazing. If you are a fan of gangsters movies such as The Godfather, Goodfellas, Pulp Fiction, Reservoir Dogs or games like Red Dead Redemption and GTA you will like this game too.

A great remake of the original game. The changes in the plot seemed good to me in some places—they fleshed out Sarah and Vincenzo—but not so much in others, where Ralph was simply pushed into the background and barely mentioned. Overall, I really enjoyed this game, and the race on Classic difficulty provides a real challenge

A friend of mine played a Mafia game and got me invested into Mafia stuff. I didn't ask him what game it was that he played, so I searched around and got this one. This wasn't the one, but I kept playing and eventually finished it. This game was awesome

A solid remaster of a classic game. Makes some minor (mostly positive) changes to the plot, but nothing crazy.
Makes some changes to generally modernise the experience.

Highest difficulty is pretty challenging, and the story pretty engaging. The latter half of the 100% (just driving around the open world picking up magazines for hours) is a bit of a slog, but almost everything has an in-game checklist, so no re-checking spots you've already been.
The exception to this is the mystery foxes, which feel rushed in in general.

An extremely well done game, the story is awesome, the gameplay is very smooth and nice, yes, even THAT mission. 

The only problem I had with classic difficulty is that enemies are really strong so some missions you have to learn the locations of enemies by dying first several times...
Such a banger game. Let's you get lost in a beautiful open world with the opportunity of beating up schmucks for cash.
italians have been extinct for over 300 years due to the great pasta famine, they have all been replaced with americans doing funny accents

-Amazing story with memorable and funny characters 
-Full of bugs (sometimes makes it frustrating)
-The gameplay is fun sometimes and just super boring other times like the stealth in this game should of been handled better but the car chases and gun fights are very fun 
-Kind of like GTA if it was in the 1930s
-7.5/10 in my opinion

I had played the OG 20 years ago and it was a huge revelation for me and many friends back then. Probably one of the best video games experience ever. The story was crazy, the gameplay too. So yeah, the bar was pretty high. 
The story has been tweaked a bit, well, why not. Graphics are very nice but pretty ugly for a 2020 game imho. 

---

What puzzles me is that many GREAT gameplay features have been dropped in this remaster : changing cars while being chase by the cops, being able to hop on the metro or tramway are a few that come to mind. 

---

Overall that remaster feels rushed and lifeless compared to the original game. Lots of driving, very little action, lots of cinematics and the scripting during mission is a bit too obvious.

Get it on sale :) Nostalgics like me, you'll probably be disapointed.
Good game, fun.Nice graphics and a hella good story.

Just finished and this is honestly one of if not the best story game ive ever played. everything is perfect. its short but everything is so well done.

An excellent remake — I played through it in one sitting. Tommy is the best protagonist of the trilogy.

Must-have for your collection when it’s on sale for $5.

The game itself runs smoothly with no drops at 144 FPS.

Main Game 5/5
Achievements 5/5
Achievements: 43/43 (100%)

Mafia: Definitive Edition was a game I went into expecting to enjoy, but I didn't expect it to become my favourite game in the series.

Growing up, Mafia II was always my Mafia game. I never played the original because even back then it felt ancient, so I had no nostalgia for Tommy Angelo's story and went into this completely fresh.

The first thing that stood out was just how good the remake looks. The graphics are fantastic, the city feels alive and the atmosphere is incredible. Whether driving through the streets during the day or cruising around at night, Lost Heaven constantly feels believable and immersive. Even when I continued my playthrough on Steam Deck while away on holiday, the game still looked fantastic and ran flawlessly.

Performance was a mixed bag initially. On PC I experienced crashes and had to spend some time tweaking settings before everything became stable. Once sorted I had very few issues, but it's worth mentioning as your experience may vary.

The story is where the game truly shines.

Tommy Angelo is a brilliant protagonist and really likeable character. The writing feels natural, the progression feels smooth and every chapter pushes the story forward. Compared to Mafia II, I found the pacing much tighter and more focused. There were very few moments where it felt like the story was dragging or jumping between events.

The supporting cast is equally strong and by the end of the game I genuinely cared about what happened to these characters.

Gameplay feels surprisingly modern. If you showed this to someone without telling them it was based on a 2002 game, they'd struggle to believe it. The driving, gunplay and general mechanics all feel great and the remake does an incredible job of modernising the original without making it feel dated.

I also have to mention the infamous race mission.

Before starting the game I'd heard endless stories about how difficult it was, especially on Classic difficulty. I decided to start the game on Classic from the beginning because it was tied to the achievement list. The race was definitely challenging, but nowhere near impossible. It took me around an hour of retries before I completed it. Later, while collecting remaining achievements on Easy difficulty, I completed it first try.

My advice would be to feather the throttle through corners rather than relying too heavily on braking, and don't be afraid to cut across the grass where possible.

Achievement-wise, this is one of the best lists I've completed. There are plenty of collectibles, but none of them felt overly grindy or disrespectful of the player's time. Starting on Classic difficulty meant I naturally worked towards several achievements from the beginning, and by the end everything felt earned without becoming tedious.

After completing the game and earning 100% of the achievements, I can honestly say this is one of the best remakes I've ever played.

The visuals are excellent, the atmosphere is incredible, the gameplay feels modern and the story is outstanding. Most importantly, it respects the source material while improving almost every aspect of it.

I wish more remakes were made to this standard.

Thinking of this game, its great story, incredible atmosphere, and remarkable immersion, every time I get recommended a Krispy Pizza shorts video on YouTube

A true classic, highly recommend
Story, characters, graphics and gameplay. 'Nuff said.

Mafia is a good game. It has a fun story, which kept me going. The graphics in the definitive edition are pretty good too. The gameplay can get a bit boring and repetitive though-there aren't a lot of options when it comes to executing the missions-its the same set of guns and a small set of stealth moves, which you need to use for some of the missions. Driving around the city is a very fun, however. It is a nice simulation of what it might have felt like to drive in the 1930s. The NPC car driving is also responsive. If you make sudden moves while driving, other cars also sway a bit and honk at you. Overall, a fun game.

Never played the classics only mafia 3 but this game is a banger and in the age for 40 + hour games this is a short but sweet game but that doesn't mean it's not as good and sometimes it's better as a lot of those 40+ hour game are just filler but Mafia is straight to the point and i like that.

no doubt, absolute masterpiece, ah the ending!!

if you didn't play it, I'm not going to play spoiler, but seriously play it was worth it and I recommend you all to play this, overall story wise 10/10 from me.
I really love this game, and the characters. The realtion between me and Paulie is now purely sexual.

Oh sweet nostalgia... They re-made the game, expanded and improved the story (and cinematics) in a good way. It stays very faithful to the original story and expands it, by rewriting (improving) dialogues and adding new, small plot-lines. I felt that I wasn't playing a re-make of a game, but the game the way I remembered it... It was a beautiful, cinematic experiences and if I wanted my friends to experience it, I would recommend the re-make instead of the original. It's not a modern open-world game, but a curated, cinematic, linear experience. It doesn't break the mould, but it's not supposed to. I love what they did with the game!

Easily the best in the series. Minimal bugs. Voice acting and cutscenes are done beautifully. The cover system and shooting are ok. Not really anything to do in the open world but that’s ok because it would just distract from the amazing story. First game on my steam deck that I picked up and played through the entire game in a weekend. Definitely recommend for anyone who is a fan of gangster movies and open world crime games
very cool game im in a reply of the series right now (played it on xbox first ) to play the new old country
S tier game design. The music and the atmosphere makes me feel i am inside old new york.
Stilll an excellent gamee, and the updates almost make it a completely new game.
A truly beautiful game. Beautiful story telling, design, gameplay. One of the best made and still plays better than the most recent games. Highly recommend.

It's far from ideal but it's a good game. Story and graphics carry this game by far. Gameplay itself is nothing to write home about. Fun driving mechanics. Okay shooting mechanics, not much to do in the world other than the main story but the story holds it's own so high up that it doesn't feel bad. Replayability is pretty much nonexistent unless you wanna try harder difficulty or just experience the story again but it's a product of love nontheless

I remember, back in the day when first "MAFIA" came out...and my friends played it.....but I was too busy.....playin' VICE CITY... ;)
All of them sayin' that MAFIA is a great game.
I didn't mind back then.

But now, after 20+ years, I've decided to play MAFIA, but DEFINITIVE EDITION, not the original one.

This game is pure perfection. Music, sounds, ambience....the whole city....you really have the feeling that you're in the 30's....
Story is basically.....GANGSTERS...
I just can't stop playing it.
Voices, radio stations, jazz....freakin' rain is perfect.

I highly recommend. Discount or not......you should all play this game.
I can't wait to launch Mafia 2 and 3....

This is pure enjoyment.

P.S. I'm still playing it......I have....4 or 5 chapters more....and couldn't wait more to share my opinion here.

To developers and creators of this game......CENT'ANNI!
Absolutely worth every single penny, this game's story and graphics are sooo amazing. One of my favorite games of all time. 100% worth buying.

The game looks and plays great. It's very linear and it doesn't feel like the typical sandbox GTA type of game but the driving it's top notch. My main complain is the shooting mechanic since weapons are not accurate at all, also the Tommy gun doesn't do much damage per bullet as compared to a pistol. Since ammo is scarce, I ended up using the semi automatic pistol most of the game, it felt the most accurate and powerfull of all weapons of the game and that says a lot.

I do not recommend playing it in higher difficulties since the AI on the other hand, have laser beam accuracy in their weapons which ends up feeling unbalaced and frustrating at times.
A great game with a solid single player story. There's some good challenge in the game (especially on classic mode), and a good variety of missions. Would recommend.
Nice game but the voice over actors from the original game are way better
Story is good. Character is awesome. Glitches during cutscene but gameplay is okay. The driving is horrendous, but the npc traffic is even worse.

Great game, great remake. Friendly vibe and a nice way to spend your time. It’s a joy to play and complete. Great characters, graphics, sound, story, and everything else. It's a wonderful experience to chill out with and complete the main story.

I love Mafia 1 so much. When I first played this game I was very impressed with everything the graphics, gameplay, characters, and most of all the story. Mafia 1 has one of the best stories that I have ever experienced and I was blown away with how good this game is. I fell in love with the characters and got attached to them really fast. I remember hearing that Mafia 2 is a masterpiece and so I was like well I will play 1 first because it's the first one and I was playing it to get to 2 but man this game is just as good I love it so much. I was so sucked in to the story that I had about 5/6 missions left and I just decided to stay up tell I was done at 5 AM and it was so worth it. The best way to describe this game is special it is so good and I highly recommend it to everyone it is a experience that you wont forget.

This game suffers from a really odd rendering issue with cutscenes where sometimes all of the audio will playout before the ctuscene has actually loaded in realtime. Other times set pieces will be missing and the scene will move ahead without them making for some extremely jarring visual storytelling. Really strange glitches for something that's not exactly hardware intensive.

Yes I would recommend this game. I played the original Mafia from 2002 a couple years ago & enjoyed it. I hear a lot of people complaining about Remake culture in video games, but I'm all for it. I enjoy a good story & this game has it. It is a linear experience but it does have an open world atmosphere. I've always been into crime gangster films & this game tells that story but during the prohibition era in the 1930's, the nostalgia definitely hits. I rate this game a 8.5/10

My interest to play this came from Berleezy's playthrough a few years back. Typically, I would just pass this by without looking back, but the story intrigued me more than anything, and I haven't been invested in a story like this in a LONG time. Might be controversial, considering that the game is a remake from the original and I've heard that some actually don't like the story, but here we go.
Tommy Angelo is such a prolific character, instantly fell in love with the background behind him, a simple taxi driver who was at the right (or wrong) place at the right or wrong time. Mr. Salieri is a strict, but initially loving boss who catches you by surprise. Ralphie is an idiot, but you gotta love him, and Sam is one of my favorite characters. To be fair, he is one of the more realistic characters of the group simply based on his actions and commitment to the mob. All the character writing is great and just keeps me wanting more.
The gameplay is fine, it is truly much more of a story-driven game rather than known for its gameplay, which I'm fine with. The story missions are fun, especially the racing one, I thought that was cool. It also provides a bit of a challenge as well, some of the missions are quite difficult (also collecting playboy magazines ;)).
That's all I got to say about this, it's a great game and I'd recommend it if you are looking for a story-driven mobster experience.
-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

pros:
- great plot and story
- amazing character development
- fun missions
- playboy magazines

cons:
- pretty short
- vehicles are a bit annoying to drive
- combat sequences can be frustrating
no deep gameplay but great story and environment and gta style play
I liked the game, the mechanics for controller could have been better.
Felt like a great mafia movie and each mission was extremely fun and different. Short game but worth it for sure

I’ve finally finished the game, and my feelings are a mix of admiration and exhaustion.
​The story, the atmosphere, and the character development are absolutely top-tier—it’s a cinematic masterpiece that keeps you hooked from start to finish. However, the gameplay itself can be incredibly clunky and frustratingly 'mechanical' at times.
​Even on the lowest difficulty, some sections (like the infamous racing sequence or certain combat encounters) feel poorly balanced and demand a level of precision that the controls don't always provide. It feels like the game design sometimes works against the player rather than with them.
​In short: if you can push through the rough gameplay, the story is 100% worth it. But be prepared to feel frustrated and stressed along the way. It’s a beautiful experience that unfortunately feels held back by its own outdated or poorly tuned mechanics.

It's absolutely insane how stunning this game is. Collecting all the stuff needed for the achievements really put into perspective how many details and areas were constructed without the expectation of anyone actually visiting them (unless they are going for 100%). Outstanding!

On a side note, that racing mission.... ugh, that bloody mission... If you played the game you know exactly what I mean. If you haven't... buona fortuna ragazzi, as Salieri used to say.

A classic. A good balance of challenge and story. Playing it for a 2nd time in 2026  having bought the original version on release. The racecar level was not as hard as I remembered.

Graphics - Good
Sound - Great

Does everything I need a game to. The only bad thing with this play through was that I already knew the story twist from 20 years ago.

For not knowing this was a remake personally, it was really fun to play. I enjoyed the combat system when i wasn't achievement hunting and the driving and racing is really really fun, especially once you get the crazy horse, MAN can that thing ZOOOOOM. had a good time 100% it once finished, but never and I mean NEVER will i EVER do that one racing mission ever again. all in all tho game was a solid 10 for me.
pretty good game but the ending killed me and the mandatory race was buns
Visually stunning, with a gripping story and smooth, enjoyable gameplay. Ran flawlessly with not a single bug throughout my entire playthrough. Highly recommended!

A very linear game that happens to have an open world.

Worth it if you just want a fun mafia story.

與其說是玩遊戲 不如說在看一場電影
經典 沉浸 很喜歡
It's a perfect example of how to bring back a classic. The game is suitable for both veterans who remember the 2002 original and newcomers looking to experience the golden age of gangster dramas.

I can't believe I've been sleeping on this game for all these years. The world, the story and the people. I've played RDR2 7 times on every system including stadia and I finally found another game to compare. Just play it. the 30s are waiting for ya.

I just finished a playthrough and my thoughts drift to, "this must've been a deal maker when released in 2002! Of course, ths is a remake published in 2020, but, you can see by the original game's storepage what was first published. The kickass story, and next gen open world graphics were there 18 yrs earlier. That story is great, a smaller Don working his way up with loyal men supporting him - good story telling is necessary to good games and Mafia (both versions) delivers!

A fantastic remaster of a classic game. Yeah the dialogue is campy and the voice acting kinda terrible, but the vision is there! Gotta play this if you haven't. I forgot how great of a job that Hanger 13 did here.

Although i didnt go for the collectibles the overall gameplay and story is great. this version of the game is basically a remake but mafia 2 definitive edition isn't a remake just an HD enhanced version
quite interesting story, great graphics. really enjoying it

Interesting game in terms of story, good graphics even after 6 years, and it's quite fun to play. From a Steam achievement hunter's perspective, they can be done easily enough with the help of a guide.

I never played these games back in the day, but I have enjoyed these remasters a great deal. They scratch the open world, narrative rich game play that is comfortable and relaxing. This is a fire it up when you want to wind down and enjoy some spare time. The missions are the right length for someone with limited play session time. The graphics and audio do a good job of getting the feel right. The voice work is a bit silly, but not immersion breaking. If you enjoyed the look and feel of LA Noire, you'll dig this one.
Great game, beautiful game not to long not to short, perfect for fulfilling the mafia dreams one may have.
Played this on xbox360 and PS3 many years ago. I also own it on PS4, great game to go back and play time to time. Awesome story and good character building throughout the game.
Very cool and atmospheric game, the start is a bit slow, but it picks up fast, a few missions are quite annoying and frustrating though, so beware

Mafia is a game close to my heart. When I heard that a remake of this legendary and groundbreaking game was in the works, I couldn’t pass it up.

The graphics are nice, and I’ve completed the game on the hardest difficulty, “Classic.” Paradoxically, it’s not that difficult to complete, but if there’s one part where you’ll definitely “hit a wall,” it’s the racing mission, which is a real test of patience on the hardest difficulty (I’m glad they stayed true to the original in this regard, where the race was also quite a challenge). In general, the driving in this game can be nerve-wracking, but you’ll get used to it over time! :D

The story is phenomenal and is guaranteed to draw you into this fictional world. After finishing the story, you’re free to roam the world at your leisure and use that time, for example, to collect all the collectibles if you’re the type of player who goes for achievements.

10/10!
Very great game the story the gameplay everything is perfect I've completed.

Mafia: Definitive Edition delivers an amazing gangster story with incredible atmosphere, music, and cinematic cutscenes that truly make you feel like you’re inside a mafia movie. The world and characters are memorable, but the gameplay itself can feel pretty mid and repetitive at times.

Even so, the story and presentation easily make it worth playing. 7/10

i really enjoyed the time i spent with mafia: definitive edition. i never played the original, but the remake was on my radar for years. i couldn't really explain why i was drawn to it, but i'm happy that i was. mafia takes place in an open world city in the 1930s. take "open world" with a grain of salt; yes the world is open, but there isn't really anything to do besides drive from point A to point B. to be fair that was actually my favorite part! stunning graphics, unique cars, and a highly immersive story are mafias strong points. gameplay was fun too but nothing to write home about. the game reminds me of some other games i love, like red dead redemption II and the yakuza series, but loosely. overall i recommend mafia to anyone who loves a good story and the ability to drive around town.

I haven’t played the original Mafia, so I can’t really compare this version to it. Taken on its own, though, Mafia: Definitive Edition is a good game with a story that kept me interested and characters I actually liked.

The game does a really nice job selling that classic mafia vibe. The atmosphere feels believable, the dialogue is well written, and the visuals still look pretty good. It has that crime drama mood down very well, without feeling too forced.

The driving, though, isn’t great. The cars feel a bit like they’re sliding around on ice, which makes some parts more annoying than they probably should be. The weapon variety is also pretty limited, but at least the shooting mechanics themselves feel solid enough.

Overall, it’s a good, atmospheric story game with strong writing and a convincing world, even if some gameplay parts don’t fully hold up.

7/10 - A good game.
story just amazing, 20 century of usa look very realsitic

As an OG Mafia 1 player, it was nice to return to New Heaven in a polished up form, and it warms my heart to see people quitting this game on the race just like the old days. I played it on Classic, skipped every trip and it was an utter walk in the park compared to the original.

This game is so different I think you should play the original as well. For a start you actually drive a lot in Mafia normally. Also gangsters usually swear and use a lot of slurs (why even change that? this game is still has you blowing up a brothel full of ♥♥♥♥♥♥). Taxi drivers usually take fares... the game made some changes that I feel aren't really improvements at all, they're more just differences. Ultimately I have to say it doesn't feel like this team was passionate about what the first game actually was. That said, they included the side missions in Free Roam and Classic difficulty with all the traffic law simulation stuff so they didn't spit on its memory or anything. And it's certainly a nice change to not have all the old jank. It's just kind of... what playing Mafia was all about. You didn't speed in a convertible because that was suicide. You'd obey traffic laws if you had to cross the entire city so you wouldnt lose health and kill your car. You'd have to bring your cars back to your garage to keep them etc. The oldest cars couldn't climb hills - you had to plan an escape route in the first level around it or you'd simply die. And you'd always be listening to that awesome radio. We got a cinematic period third person shooter, but we kind of lost the period open city.

I did try to drive a bit to see if the conversations that normally happen when you're driving with passengers were still in the game, but i didn't hear much, and the news reports about my actions were simply boring. Letting us ride the taxis to quick travel would have been a better way to handle it imo. Trip Skip as a feature was always supposed to be for repeating failed missions. Thanks to the never-drive button, I don't even know if Luca's missions are still in the game, or if it's just the postcard cars, because I literally never drove from one place to another in the story.

Another small thing, I know it wasn't really in the original game either, but uh... 1930s america was racially segregated. maybe im blind, i didnt exactly explore much, but there didn't seem to be much evidence of that in this game. The whole thing feels very santized considering the subject matter. I would have thought including that aspect was relevant to the next two games, which directly mention black civil rights.

Almost forgot I was playing a Mafia game with how ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ long its taken to beat that stupid ass racing level.

Apart from that, 10/10 .

The only thing bad about this game is the final titles song. Like, come on. Otherwise - the story, the characters, the ending, the sound design, the car handling, the difficulty - everything is great.

Mafia: Definitive Edition is a game that really stands out because of its incredible story.

The gameplay itself won’t blow anyone away, but it doesn’t really need to. The main strength here is the combination of a completely rebuilt modern visual style and a timeless, well-written narrative that carries the entire experience.

The combat is solid and gets the job done, but it’s not the reason you play this game. You play it for the story, the atmosphere, and the characters and in that regard, it delivers perfectly.

It’s a true classic that proves how powerful storytelling in games can still be, even today.

Definitely worth your time.

Mafia is a cinematic, emotional masterpiece that perfectly captures the tragedy of a life of crime. Its unforgettable narrative and gripping, heart-wrenching ending make it an absolute must-play experience for any fan of storytelling in games.

This game was incredible. The story is good, the gameplay is smooth thanks to the remaster (although I never played the original), and it had a good balance of cinema. Finishing on that final cutscene was awesome. This is now one of my favourite games.

For me, this Is the best open world and story rich game.
The remake Is something else. We Needed It and it turned into one of the best videogame remake ever.
If you are looking foward to a story rich game, this Is It.
It basicly GTA + 1930.
One of the best videogames ive player in a long time.
9.5/10

Great remake, they really went all out on the graphics as it looks amazing. Combat is improved and the race of doom is still an absolute b*tch.
Only complaint is the voice acting, Paulie and Sam is a bit over the top with the accents, while Don Salierie looks and sounds like he's from this century.

If you love mobster movies or story-driven games, this is an absolute must-play.
7.8/10 (pretty short tho)
One of good game, just finish all achivements today and pretty sad its over. Classic difficulty is indeed a challenge in this game. Got killed every time but still enjoy it, recomended play this

Fantastic game with a great atmosphere, strong story, and memorable characters that make Lost Heaven feel alive from the start. The visuals are excellent, the driving and shooting feel good, and the whole game has a classy old school gangster charm. The only thing worth mentioning is the race mission on Classic difficulty, which feels less like a normal level and more like a cheerful little exercise in gamer masochism, but surviving it  makes the victory even sweeter. I definitely recommend this game.
good, enthralling story at times challenging fights depending  on what difficulty

30 minutes into gameplay and it crashed. not a whole lot crashes on my PC but this game did, I want to play but not if that happens a lot. going back in for take two. I sit idle so 3.6 hours of gameplay is actually like 45 minutes

logged back in after crash the controller needs to be on before the game launches 3/10 for convenience.
gameplay at 45 minutes 7.3/10 some controls are wacky. story thus far 8.7/10

10/10

Fantastic story, fun driving, beautiful open world, cars and graphics.

Pros: looks great, atmosphere is great, city, cars, visuals. Runs on linux (although with some glitching); Cinematics, story, voice acting all very good.
Cons: story is only about 14h long. For me game froze about every 2 missions and needed a cold restart. Thankfully, checkpoint autosave made it bearable.
A classic story. Not an amazing remake but gets the job done to bring back the game to the new generation.

Ice Cube sends his regards..

A must play game if you consider yourself a player of games.

La historia es buenísima, te atrapa y te hace sumergirte y querer saber que es lo siguiente que va a pasar. 
Es un juego y una historia para jugarla despacio metiéndote en la piel de estos mafiosos y prestando atención a literalmente todo lo que dicen hasta los NPCs cuándo están hablando con otros.
the story of this game is very cinimatic and fentastic

I went into Mafia under the assumption that it was just one of many GTA clones only to discover it was actually not even open world and instead, a narrative focused level based game with a large (unlike a game named grand theft auto which oddly enough doesn't make driving a major focus and is more of just a thing you can do) focus on driving cars. This lead me to actually enjoy the game as i found the driving to actually feel good for once and the heavy "realistic" weapons to be fun to shoot. 

I kind of don't care about the story at all though? It's just like every single mafia based anything ever. Yeah a bunch of guys who's names end with Y or something phonetically similar at the very least do crimes and uh oh there's drama and infighting and betrayal and some of the Y guys die and some of the Y guys actually had names ending in IE this whole time and you eat a da pizza and drive a da car and... be racist towards the Chinese? I guess? Turns out mafia guys can be problematic who knew. It's very predictable standard mafia shenanigans but majority of it is really just "We are mafia guys here why we gotta smash this guys bones and steal his car" and interestingly unlike most games of the style never wasted time with just having me follow a slow mafioso for an extended period of time or do nothing while someone tells me something i already know and instead just let me shoot people and drive the cool cars so, it gets a pass from me. 

This really is a car game though, if you do not want to drive old clunky cars that often top out at just above 60 you will hate this. If you do not want to participate in races where a single mistake is a death sentence forcing a full restart, you will hate this. If you do want to drive said cars and manually shift gears and whatever car things you may be asked to do whilst simultaneously being Italian and possibly also shooting a gun while you do it, you will probably find some degree of whimsy and joy with in.

I've played, I guess around 60% of the game.  
I just abandonned after suffering and raging through the experience so far.  
Horrendous performances and bad game design is plaguing this game.  

It is a "remake" and I would expect this kind of things to be pretty much ironed out.  
Still, it is an exercise in frustration.  

The story itself is correct, I would say.
This is not "The Godfather II", "Miller's Crossing", "Once Upon a Time in America", "Boardwalk Empire" or any James Cagney movie of the 1930s.
Still, if it was not for the bugs, glitches, lag and all around stupid design ideas, it would be pretty cool to immerse yourself in the 1930s crime scene.  

Just skip this one.
Even if it is a bargain.

I enjoyed this game quite a lot, it has a chapter based version of story telling instead of free roam like GTA but it was still very good. The graphics still hold up really well, but the story telling is what really sells it. Every chapter starts and ends with cut scenes with beautiful animations that make you really feel like you're living that story.

I experienced a few minor glitches, as in any other game, but nothing serious outside of one game crash but I think that was another program I had minimized instead of closing in the background. My only real complaint being pushed to log in to 2K servers on game boot up. I wasn't forced to create an account, but it still checks in on some server somewhere so eventually one day that server might not be there .. which means the game does have an expiration date.

Story-based game. I thought it would be similar to GTA, but it wasn’t — and I’m happy it wasn’t, because this turned out to be a great 10-hour game with an amazing story. The graphics are pretty good, and the combat system is kind of unique because you can’t just rush in and shoot everything. The story is very unexpected and unique as well.

Best mafia game tbh. It really hit different for me after playing Mafia II: Definitive Edition first. The references and especially the ending gave me chills.

I found myself replaying chapters just to catch everything again. The chemistry between Tommy, Paulie, and Sam is really cool and makes the story feel alive.

Soundtrack is great too, it just ties everything together perfectly. I’m genuinely recommending it.

Absolute Cinema, I really mean it.

I never knew I was gonna get so hooked on this game.
The graphics is stunning even to today's standard and the story is absolutely amazing.
Although I experienced a bit of bugs, but it didn't really bother me.

10/10 would recommend, great remake overall.

I get that this franchise has a lot of fans but this game looks like it was made in 2015 and plays like it was released in 2002. Awful controls, particularly the shooting, melee, walking, running and driving (basically everything) — story doesn’t make up for it. If you really want to play it, just make sure it’s a great sale.

Same mission all over again, takes forever to reload a weapon, game is full of glitches. Like if you attack enemy behind their back they wont shoot at you even if they see you, bullets are big problem, for people you need to shot you get like 18 bullets, and you can not collect every gun of your enemy. BS

Installed it after a couple of suggestions. How embarassing that, after all this time, the game still isn't running correctly on a steam deck.

Игра неиронично на экране перезапуска первым вариантом предлагает выбрать "с начала главы" и это может сыграть очень злую шутку с вами, когда этот рестарт происходит уже 5-10-5012304512 раз и можно просто случайно не увидев ткнуть и проебать весь прогресс.

ангар 13, вы как всегда в своём стиле (♥♥♥♥♥ делаете) делаете мелочи, которые портят весь опыт от игры.

This game has a very good story behind this, I have to say this is such an amazing game and it was truly awesome to play it. The characters are great, gameplay is good and its a good 10-12 hours. If you go for the more difficult options, you could see around 15-16 hours. Overall I would highly recommend this game to others!
